q: Which correctly describes top-down versus bottom-up population control?
options: Top-down: producers control herbivore numbers; bottom-up: predators control prey
Top-down: predators regulate prey populations; bottom-up: resource availability (e.g., plant productivity) limits herbivore and higher trophic level populations
Both terms describe the same thing — negative feedback control of population size by abiotic factors
Top-down control is driven by nutrient cycling; bottom-up control is driven by carnivore abundance
explanation: Top-down control is predator-driven (wolves limit deer); bottom-up control is resource-driven (grass productivity limits the entire food web above it).
